mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 1.0% and exhibits a declining trend, signaling monetary accommodation. This low-rate environment reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of April 2022 indicates accommodative stance
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a 2-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and recession risk. Equity investors reprrice risk premiums upward and reduce equity allocations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 5.0% in any 2-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of April 2022, near threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 5-day SPX returns is positive (r > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate, indicating weakening aggregate demand and reduced corporate earnings growth expectations. Equity valuations compress as growth expectations fall, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% in any quarter
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of quarters where GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, or Spearman rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 8.0%, signaling stagflation. Stagflation reduces real returns on equity and increases equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.226 and CPIAUCSL at 241.002 as of March 2022
regime_hint: stagflation regime with supply constraints and high inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 62% of instances where both conditions are met, or the joint effect of INDPRO decline and CPIAUCSL > 8.0% does not reduce forward returns relative to baseline (ANCOVA p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1000 thousand units annualized, indicating weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic weakness. Declining housing demand signals reduced consumer wealth and future consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 thousand units in any month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1047.0 thousand units as of March 2022, near threshold
regime_hint: demand-driven slowdown with housing market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of months where HOUST < 1000, or Rank IC between HOUST and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100 thousand jobs per month, signaling labor market weakness and reduced consumer income growth. Weakening labor demand reduces earnings growth expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100 thousand jobs
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144952.0 thousand as of April 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of months where PAYEMS growth < 100k, or correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (r < -0.08, p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, indicating joint deterioration in consumer demand and labor market. This dual weakness signals reduced corporate earnings and increased recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below 0.2% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 465914.0M and UNRATE at 4.9% as of April 2022
regime_hint: demand-driven slowdown with synchronized consumer and labor weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction effect (RSAFS × UNRATE) does not significantly improve model fit relative to additive effects (F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate rises above 1.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 8.0%, signaling monetary tightening in a high-inflation regime. Tightening monetary policy increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% and CPIAUCSL at 241.002 as of April 2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent high inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where both conditions are met, or the joint effect of FEDFUNDS > 1.0% and CPIAUCSL > 8.0% does not reduce forward returns relative to single-factor models (likelihood ratio test p > 0.12).
